titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Académie française autograph collection
titlestmt.author: Karen Spicher

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12449085.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 29, 2017 at 08:52

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence and other documents signed by circa 200 members of the Académie française, 1637-1926 and undated, assembled by an unidentified collector, possibly American autograph collector Howes Norris, Jr. (1867-1938), of Vineyard Haven, Massachusetts. Most are autograph letters, signed; other contents include literary writings, legal documents, and a few documents concerning administration of the Académie française. Many are accompanied by descriptions from vendor catalogs, engraved portraits, clippings, or notes of former owners. Correspondence includes an autograph letter, signed, from Alexis de Tocqueville to Robert Walsh, consul general of the United States in Paris, 1845 June 10.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Goodspeed's Book Shop on an unknown fund, 1954.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Académie française Autograph Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Académie française Autograph Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat Ms Vault 139. In 2015, the collection received a baseline level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Most folder titles were based on those assigned by Library staff at the time of acquisition. Names were updated to conform to Library of Congress name authorities. Folder titles were not verified against contents.

bioghist.head: Académie française
bioghist.p: Learned society for the French language, founded in 1635 by Richelieu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and other documents signed by circa 200 members of the Académie française, 1637-1926 and undated, assembled by an unidentified collector, possibly American autograph collector Howes Norris, Jr. (1867-1938), of Vineyard Haven, Massachusetts. Most are autograph letters, signed; other contents include literary writings, legal documents, and a few documents concerning administration of the Académie française. Many are accompanied by descriptions from vendor catalogs, engraved portraits, clippings, or notes of former owners. Correspondence includes an autograph letter, signed, from Alexis de Tocqueville to Robert Walsh, consul general of the United States in Paris, 1845 June 10. Documents relating to Maurice Donnay and Albert, comte de Mun, include signatures on printed cards identified as "The autograph collection of Howes Norris, Jr."

did.unittitle: Argenson, Marc-Pierre de Voyer, comte d', 1696-1764
Audiffret-Pasquier, Edme Armand Gaston, duc de, 1823-1905
Belle-Isle, Charles Louis Auguste Fouquet, duc de, 1684-1761
Buffon, Georges Louis Leclerc, comte de, 1707-1788

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The autograph collection of Howes Norris, Jr."

did.unittitle: Duras, Emmanuel-Céleste-Augustin de Durfort, duc de, 1741-1800
Empis, M. (Adolphe Dominique Florent Joseph Simonis), 1795-1868
Estrées, Louis-Charles-César Le Tellier, duc d', 1695-1771
Falloux du Coudray, Alfred-Frédéric-Pierre, comte de, 1811-1886
François de Neufchâteau, Nicolas Louis, comte, 1750-1828
La Cépède, M. le comte de (Bernard Germain Etienne de La Ville sur Illon), 1756-1825
La Force, Henri-Jacques-Nompar de Caumont, duc de, 1675-1726
Lalande, Joseph Jérôme Le Français de, 1732-1807

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The autograph collection of Howes Norris, Jr." Includes photograph of Mun

did.unittitle: Pastoret, Claude Emmanuel Joseph Pierre, marquis de, 1755-1840
Richelieu, Louis François Armand du Plessis, duc de, 1696-1788

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appended to letter from Louis de Pardaillan de Gondrin, 1731 February 23

did.unittitle: Sainte-Aulaire, comte de (Louis Clair de Beaupoil), 1778-1854
Suard, J. B. A. (Jean Baptiste Antoine), 1734-1817

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph letter, signed, to Robert Walsh, consul general of the United States in Paris
